steps.title: Translate the word problem into an inequality
steps.content:
The service fee is a fixed cost of $25.
The hourly cost is $10 per hour for $t$ hours, giving $10t$.
Total cost = $25 + 10t$.
The person wants to spend at most \$75$, so we set up the inequality $$25 + 10t \le 75$$.

steps.title: Match the inequality to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice A is $10t \le 75$, missing the service fee.
Choice B is $10 + 25t \le 75$, swapping the coefficients.
Choice C is $25t \le 75$, using the wrong variable for the hourly rate.
Choice D is $25 + 10t \le 75$, exactly the inequality derived.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
